name: Lowfat Fettuccine Alfredo with Asparagus
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups asparagus spears with tight tips, sliced diagonally, tough ends removed
1 teaspoon baked garlic (about 2 cloves), mashed and chopped
1 1/2 cups skim milk or more as needed
2 tablespoons light process cream cheese product (NOT nonfat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a medium-size nonstick saute pan over medium heat and spray with vegetable oil spray. Remove from the heat.
- Add the onion and saute until limp, about 5 to 10 minutes.
- Add asparagus and garlic, cover the pan, and turn off the heat. Steam from the onion will cook the asparagus.
2. In a large skillet, combine dry milk and skim milk and whisk until blended. Stir in flour, cooking over medium-high heat until thickened.
- In a small bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of hot sauce with cream cheese (NOT nonfat) until smooth. Return this mixture to the hot sauce. Add Parmesan and stir until melted. Keep hot. If thick, thin with skim milk; consistency should be like cream.
3. Cook fettuccine in boiling water according to package directions until al dente. Drain.
- Stir heated pasta, garlic, and vegetables into sauce in skillet over medium-low heat until heated through.
- Serve garnished with chopped arugula.
NOTE: To bake garlic:
- Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
- Place whole head of garlic in a baking pan, drizzle with olive oil, add water. Bake loosely covered for 45 to 60 minutes or until cloves are soft.
- Use mashed garlic in dishes or serve whole as side dish with roast meat.
